children.text: Demonstration of DOCX support in calibre
children.rawContent: Demonstration of DOCX support in calibre

children.text: This document demonstrates the ability of the calibre DOCX Input plugin to convert the various typographic features in a Microsoft Word (2007 and newer) document. Convert this document to a modern ebook format, such as AZW3 for Kindles or EPUB for other ebook readers, to see it in action.
children.rawContent: This document demonstrates the ability of the calibre DOCX Input plugin to convert the various typographic features in a Microsoft Word (2007 and newer) document. Convert this document to a modern ebook format, such as AZW3 for Kindles or EPUB for other ebook readers, to see it in action.

children.text: There is support for images, tables, lists, footnotes, endnotes, links, dropcaps and various types of text and paragraph level formatting.
children.rawContent: There is support for images, tables, lists, footnotes, endnotes, links, dropcaps and various types of text and paragraph level formatting.

children.text: To see the DOCX conversion in action, simply add this file to calibre using the
children.rawContent: To see the DOCX conversion in action, simply add this file to calibre using the

children.text: Set the output format in the top right corner of the conversion dialog to EPUB or AZW3 and click
children.rawContent: Set the output format in the top right corner of the conversion dialog to EPUB or AZW3 and click

children.text: Here, we demonstrate various types of inline text formatting and the use of embedded fonts.
children.rawContent: Here, we demonstrate various types of inline text formatting and the use of embedded fonts.

children.text: text. Then, we have a super
children.rawContent: text. Then, we have a super

children.text: . This paragraph uses document wide styles for styling rather than inline text properties as demonstrated in the previous paragraph — calibre can handle both with equal ease.
children.rawContent: . This paragraph uses document wide styles for styling rather than inline text properties as demonstrated in the previous paragraph — calibre can handle both with equal ease.

children.text: This document has embedded the Ubuntu font family. The body text is in the Ubuntu typeface, here is
children.rawContent: This document has embedded the Ubuntu font family. The body text is in the Ubuntu typeface, here is

children.text: some text in the Ubuntu Mono typeface, notice how every letter has the same width, even i and m
children.rawContent: some text in the Ubuntu Mono typeface, notice how every letter has the same width, even i and m

children.text: . Every embedded font will automatically be embedded in the output ebook during conversion.
children.rawContent: . Every embedded font will automatically be embedded in the output ebook during conversion.

children.text: You can do crazy things with paragraphs, if the urge strikes you. For instance this paragraph is right aligned and has a right border. It has also been given a light gray background.
children.rawContent: You can do crazy things with paragraphs, if the urge strikes you. For instance this paragraph is right aligned and has a right border. It has also been given a light gray background.

children.text: For the lovers of poetry amongst you, paragraphs with hanging indents, like this often come in handy. You can use hanging indents to ensure that a line of poetry retains its individual identity as a line even when the screen is too narrow to display it as a single line. Not only does this paragraph have a hanging indent, it is also has an extra top margin, setting it apart from the preceding paragraph.
children.rawContent: For the lovers of poetry amongst you, paragraphs with hanging indents, like this often come in handy. You can use hanging indents to ensure that a line of poetry retains its individual identity as a line even when the screen is  too narrow to display it as a single line. Not only does this paragraph have a hanging indent, it is also has an extra top margin, setting it apart from the preceding paragraph.

children.text: Tables in Word can vary from the extremely simple to the extremely complex. calibre tries to do its best when converting tables. While you may run into trouble with the occasional table, the vast majority of common cases should be converted very well, as demonstrated in this section. Note that for optimum results, when creating tables in Word, you should set their widths using percentages, rather than absolute units. To the left of this paragraph is a floating two column table with a nice green border and header row.
children.rawContent: Tables in Word can vary from the extremely simple to the extremely complex. calibre tries to do its best when converting tables. While you may run into trouble with the occasional table, the vast majority of common cases should be converted very well, as demonstrated in this section. Note that for optimum results, when creating tables in Word, you should set their widths using percentages, rather than absolute units.  To the left of this paragraph is a floating two column table with a nice green border and header row.

children.text: Now let’s look at a fancier table—one with alternating row colors and partial borders. This table is stretched out to take 100% of the available width.
children.rawContent: Now let’s look at a fancier table—one with alternating row colors and partial borders. This table is stretched out to take 100% of the available width.

children.text: Next, we see a table with special formatting in various locations. Notice how the formatting for the header row and sub header rows is preserved.
children.rawContent: Next, we see a table with special formatting in various locations. Notice how the formatting for the header row and sub header rows is preserved.

children.text: Fictitious data, for illustration purposes only
children.rawContent: Fictitious data, for illustration purposes only

children.text: Next, we have something a little more complex, a nested table, i.e. a table inside another table. Additionally, the inner table has some of its cells merged. The table is displayed horizontally centered.
children.rawContent: Next, we have something a little more complex, a nested table, i.e. a table inside another table. Additionally, the inner table has some of its cells merged. The table is displayed horizontally centered.

children.text: To the left is a table inside a table, with some cells merged.
children.rawContent: To the left is a table inside a table, with some cells merged.

children.text: We end with a fancy calendar, note how much of the original formatting is preserved. Note that this table will only display correctly on relatively wide screens. In general, very wide tables or tables whose cells have fixed width requirements don’t fare well in ebooks.
children.rawContent: We end with a fancy calendar, note how much of the original formatting is preserved. Note that this table will only display correctly on relatively wide screens. In general, very wide tables or tables whose cells have fixed width requirements don’t fare well in ebooks.

children.text: Miscellaneous structural elements you can add to your document, like footnotes, endnotes, dropcaps and the like.
children.rawContent: Miscellaneous structural elements you can add to your document, like footnotes, endnotes, dropcaps and the like.

children.text: are automatically recognized and both are converted to endnotes, with backlinks for maximum ease of use in ebook devices.
children.rawContent: are automatically recognized and both are converted to endnotes, with backlinks for maximum ease of use in ebook devices.

children.text: rop caps are used to emphasize the leading paragraph at the start of a section. In Word it is possible to specify how many lines of text a drop-cap should use.
children.rawContent: rop caps are used to emphasize the leading paragraph at the start of a section. In Word it is possible to specify how many lines of text a drop-cap should use.

children.text: Two kinds of links are possible, those that refer to an external website and those that refer to locations inside the document itself. Both are supported by calibre. For example, here is a link pointing to the
children.rawContent: Two kinds of links are possible, those that refer to an external website and those that refer to locations inside the document itself. Both are supported by calibre. For example, here is a link pointing to the

children.text: . Then we have a link that points back to the section on

children.text: You can see the Table of Contents created by calibre by clicking the Table of Contents button in whatever viewer you are using to view the converted ebook.
children.rawContent: You can see the Table of Contents created by calibre by clicking the Table of Contents button in whatever viewer you are using to view the converted ebook.

children.text: Demonstration of DOCX support in calibre
children.rawContent: Demonstration of DOCX support in calibre

children.text: Centered images like this are useful for large pictures that should be a focus of attention.
children.rawContent: Centered images like this are useful for large pictures that should be a focus of attention.

children.text: There is no analogous technology in ebooks, so the conversion will usually end up placing the image either centered or floating close to the point in the text where it was
children.rawContent: There is no analogous technology in ebooks, so the conversion will usually end up placing the image either centered or floating close to the point in the text where it was

children.text: , not necessarily where it appears on the page in Word.
children.rawContent: , not necessarily where it appears on the page in Word.

children.text: All types of lists are supported by the conversion, with the exception of lists that use fancy bullets, these get converted to regular bullets.
children.rawContent: All types of lists are supported by the conversion, with the exception of lists that use fancy bullets, these get converted to regular bullets.

children.text: An interruption in our regularly scheduled listing, for this essential and very relevant public service announcement.

content.text: We now resume our normal programming

children.text: We now resume our normal programming

children.text: In paged media, footnotes are usually displayed at the bottom of the text. However, in ebooks, a better paradigm is to make them clickable endnotes that the user can browse at her pleasure. This conversion is handled automatically by calibre.
children.rawContent: In paged media, footnotes are usually displayed at the bottom of the text. However, in ebooks, a better paradigm is to make them clickable endnotes that the user can browse at her pleasure. This conversion is handled automatically by calibre.

children.text: Endnotes are typically used for longer notes, they remain endnotes when converted into ebook form, except that they have an additional backlink to make it easy to return to the current position after reading the note.
children.rawContent: Endnotes are typically used for longer notes, they remain endnotes when converted into ebook form, except that they have an additional backlink to make it easy to return to the current position after reading the note.
